title: fix: wan-image models ignore width/height parameters
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Thinking outside the square
gist.blurb: The Wan image models were stubbornly making everything square. We've fixed the math so your requested aspect ratios actually work now.
gist.summary: Fixed an issue where Wan image models ignored user-requested width and height parameters by passing explicit pixel dimensions instead of resolution presets.
gist.impact: Users will now receive images in their requested aspect ratios instead of being forced into square outputs when using Wan models.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot working together to stretch a square canvas into a rectangle, representing the fix for aspect ratios.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky monitor-headed robot working together to stretch a glowing square canvas into a wide rectangle. The canvas shows a beautiful pixel landscape. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and peach gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and a chunky monitor-headed robot working together to stretch a glowing square canvas into a wide rectangle. The canvas shows a beautiful pixel landscape. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and peach gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
